channel.title: ThreatDigest Hub - Curated Cyber Incidents
channel.description: A curated list of recent cyber incidents, attacks, and security threats.

item.title: [DARK WEB] ThreatFox: Meterpreter — 10 new IOCs detected
item.description:
ThreatFox Database
Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) on ThreatFox are associated with a certain malware fas. A malware sample can be associated with only one malware family. The page below gives you an overview on indicators of compromise associated with meterpreter .

ThreatFox Database
Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) on ThreatFox are associated with a certain malware fas. A malware sample can be associated with only one malware family. The page below gives you an overview on indicators of compromise associated with strelastealer .

item.title: Zero trust, zero buzzwords: Here’s what it means
item.description: In this Help Net Security video, Murat Balaban, CEO of Zenarmor, breaks down zero trust and zero trust network access (ZTNA) without the buzzwords. The video covers why this approach matters, including the risk of lateral movement after a breach and the growing number of remote workers accessing private resources. Murat walks through three real-world scenarios: stolen VPN credentials, a compromised employee laptop, and third-party contractor access.

item.title: March 2026 Patch Tuesday: Eight Critical Vulnerabilities and Two Publicly Disclosed Among 82 CVEs Patched
item.description:
Critical Vulnerability in Microsoft Devices Pricing Program
CVE-2026-21536 is a Critical remote code execution vulnerability affecting Microsoft Devices Pricing Program and has a CVSS score of 9.8. This vulnerability enables unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting an unrestricted file upload weakness (CWE-434) in the Microsoft Devices Pricing Program, which fails to properly validate uploaded file types. The vulnerability requires no user interaction and has ...

item.title: Responding to a data breach in Jamaica
item.description: Akil Williams. TODAY, personal data has become one of the most valuable assets for organisations. Businesses, government agencies, financial institutions, and service providers collect large amounts of personal data daily, ranging from simple identifiers such as a customer’s name, phone number, and address to sensitive information like health records and biometric data.
